positiveTriggers: Design or change an automated delivery pipeline, quality gate, staged deployment, or release automation.
negativeTriggers: A currently failing hosted CI run needs provider-specific diagnosis rather than pipeline design.
contextRequirements: Repository delivery target, test commands, environments, and deployment constraints are available.
inputs: Repository delivery requirements and current automation.
outputs: Reviewed pipeline design or configuration with gates and rollback.
sideEffects: May change repository CI configuration after authorization.
validation: Pipeline syntax and representative gates run successfully in the target environment.
permissionsRequired: Repository write; deployment access only when explicitly authorized.
fallback: Use native planning for a read-only pipeline proposal; use a visible provider diagnostic capability for failing runs.
humanConfirmWhen: Before writing CI configuration, changing secrets, publishing, or deploying.
agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
optional tools are capability-checked at runtime

positiveTriggers: Replace, consolidate, retire, or migrate an API, dependency, feature, system, or data contract.

validation: Compatibility tests, observability, adoption evidence, and rollback criteria close.
permissionsRequired: Read access for planning; write/migration authority only when explicit.
fallback: Keep the old contract active and produce a read-only migration proposal.

negativeTriggers: A known implementation task with no failure symptom should use normal coding or TDD.

sideEffects: May add temporary instrumentation or change code after authorization.
validation: Original symptom reproduces before the fix and the regression test passes after it.
permissionsRequired: Read access by default; write or production instrumentation requires authorization.
fallback: Narrow uncertainty and request the missing reproduction or evidence.

positiveTriggers: Stress-test a plan against existing domain language, ADRs, constraints, and documented decisions.
Clarify whether a document is current authority, historical context, generated projection, or working notes before editing.
A product-discovery plan or sensitive document needs questions that separate evidence, assumptions, and authority boundaries.
negativeTriggers: A casual question or plan without a documentation/domain context can use native discussion.
contextRequirements: Relevant domain documents and the decision under review are available.
inputs: Plan or design plus CONTEXT, ADR, glossary, or equivalent domain records.
outputs: Resolved ambiguities and reviewed updates to domain/decision documents.
ADR or documentation update proposal with context, decision, alternatives, consequences, status, supersession path, and evidence when warranted.
Discovery or personal-document boundary notes that avoid invented research, participant data, legal claims, employment claims, or unsupported durable statements.
sideEffects: May update documentation only after the user approves the evolving decisions.
validation: Terms, decisions, unresolved branches, and document changes are internally consistent.
Generated projections, handoff notes, and working notes are not promoted to authority without explicit responsible-subject acceptance.
Research, personal, legal, employment, privacy, and identity-sensitive claims remain marked until the responsible subject accepts them.
permissionsRequired: Read domain documents; write permission for documentation updates.
fallback: Run a read-only challenge and return proposed document changes.
humanConfirmWhen: Before changing authoritative domain or architecture decisions.
agentCompatibility: cross-agent instruction following
